title: The Truth Feels Better
lesson: Telling the truth after a mistake mends things and makes you feel lighter inside.
prompts: In a cozy living room in the afternoon — a wooden shelf on the wall, a wooden floor, warm afternoon light — the wooden shelf holds a plain glossy blue ceramic flower vase, and the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image is on the wooden floor, one hand pushing a red ball down in mid-bounce with the ball just below the open hand, facing the camera with their face fully visible and a playful expression. Medium shot at eye level, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a cozy living room in the afternoon — a wooden shelf on the wall, a wooden floor, warm afternoon light — the wooden shelf is completely bare with nothing standing on it, and scattered flat on the wooden floor below it are small scattered blue ceramic shards lying flat (no whole or rounded vase anywhere in frame), none larger than a coin, with a little spilled water; the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands among them facing the camera, both hands flying up beside their cheeks with fingers spread and eyes wide in shock, the mouth and whole face left fully visible and unobstructed. Medium shot at eye level, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a cozy living room in the afternoon — a wooden shelf on the wall, a wooden floor, warm afternoon light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands alone beside small scattered blue ceramic shards lying flat on the wooden floor (no whole or rounded vase anywhere in frame), the wooden shelf bare above them, one hand pressed to their cheek, looking down worriedly but with the head turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible. Eye-level medium shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a cozy living room in the afternoon — a wooden shelf on the wall, a wooden floor, warm afternoon light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left, facing the camera with an honest, slightly nervous expression and the face fully visible, one arm extended low and the index finger clearly pointing down at small scattered blue ceramic shards lying flat on the wooden floor (no whole or rounded vase anywhere in frame). On the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} kneels down to their level, listening with a calm, understanding look. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a cozy living room in the afternoon — a wooden shelf on the wall, a wooden floor, warm afternoon light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image is on the far left, kneeling to pick up small scattered blue ceramic shards lying flat on the wooden floor (no whole or rounded vase anywhere in frame) but with the head turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible, with a softer expression. {GENDER_F_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} kneels to the right, helping gather the pieces and smiling gently. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a cozy living room in the afternoon — a wooden shelf on the wall, a wooden floor, warm afternoon light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left, one hand resting over their heart in relief, facing the camera with a happy smile and the face fully visible. On the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} rests a hand warmly on their shoulder. A fresh flower sits in a new cup on the wooden shelf behind them. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.
gists: In a cozy afternoon living room with a wooden shelf holding a blue ceramic vase, the child plays alone on the floor bouncing a red ball, playful.
In the living room, the wooden shelf is now bare and the blue vase lies shattered in shards on the floor while the child stands alone among them, hands flying up beside their cheeks in shock.
In the living room beside the shattered blue shards with the shelf bare above, the child stands alone, hand to cheek, looking down worriedly — unsure what to do.
In the living room, the child (far left) points down at the shattered blue shards with an honest, nervous look while the mother at right kneels to their level, listening calmly — the child owns up.

In the living room, the child (far left) kneels helping pick up the blue shards with a softer expression while the mother at right kneels gathering pieces too, smiling gently — telling the truth, cleaning up together.

In the living room with a fresh flower in a new cup on the shelf, the child (far left) rests a hand over their heart in relief, happy, while the mother at right rests a hand on their shoulder — the truth feels lighter.
texts: You were bouncing your red ball in the living room. Boing, boing — what fun!
Then crash! The blue vase fell and broke into pieces. You gasped and covered your mouth.
You looked at the broken pieces and felt worried inside. What should you do?
You found Mum and pointed to the vase. "I broke it. I'm sorry," you said honestly.
"Thank you for telling the truth," said Mum gently, and together you picked up the pieces.
Mum gave your shoulder a warm pat, and you smiled with relief. The truth made you feel light inside.
